title: नाग स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री नाग स्तोत्रम् ॥

॥ ध्यानम् ॥
फणामणिविभूषितं कनकवर्णमुज्ज्वलम्।
विषाननं महोग्रं च सर्पराजं नमाम्यहम्॥

॥ स्तोत्रम् ॥
सर्पराजो महासर्पः शेषो वासुकिस्तथा।
तक्षको नागराजश्च कार्कोटोऽथ धनञ्जयः॥ १॥

कालियः पद्मनाभश्च शंखपालो महाबलः।
एते नवनागेन्द्राः सर्वे शिवगणाः स्मृताः॥ २॥

अनन्तं वासुकिं शेषं पद्मनाभं च कम्बलम्।
शंखपालं धार्तराष्ट्रं तक्षकं कालियं तथा॥ ३॥

एतानि नवनामानि नागानां प्रातरुत्थाय यः पठेत्।
तस्य नाग भयं नास्ति सर्वत्र विजयी भवेत्॥ ४॥

॥ नागदेवता स्तुति ॥
नमोऽस्तु सर्पेभ्यो ये के च पृथिवीमनु।
ये अन्तरिक्षे ये दिवि तेभ्यः सर्पेभ्यो नमः॥ ५॥

ये नागाः पाताले लोके ये नागाः सागरे स्थिताः।
ये नागाः पर्वते वृक्षे ये नागाः ऋषिसेविताः॥ ६॥

तेभ्यः सर्वेभ्यो नमो नमो नागेभ्यो भुजगेश्वरेभ्यः।
नागलोकपतिभ्यश्च तेभ्यो नित्यं नमो नमः॥ ७॥

॥ शेषनाग स्तुति ॥
सहस्रशीर्षं देवं विश्वाक्षं विश्वशम्भुवम्।
शेषं शान्तमनन्तं च विष्णुशय्यां नमाम्यहम्॥ ८॥

यस्योरगेन्द्रस्य शिरःसु पृथिवी स्थिता।
नमामि तमनन्तं च शिवं शेषं सुमङ्गलम्॥ ९॥

॥ नागपूजा मन्त्र ॥
ॐ नमो भगवते वासुकये नमः।
ॐ नमो भगवते तक्षकाय नमः।
ॐ नमो भगवते शेषाय नमः।
ॐ नमो भगवते कार्कोटकाय नमः।
ॐ नमो भगवते पद्माय नमः।
ॐ नमो भगवते महापद्माय नमः।
ॐ नमो भगवते शंखाय नमः।
ॐ नमो भगवते कुलिकाय नमः॥

॥ काल सर्प शान्ति मन्त्र ॥
ॐ नागेभ्यो नमः। ॐ सर्पेभ्यो नमः।
सर्वनागप्रीतये सर्वसर्पभयनिवारणाय
नागराजशेषाय नमो नमः॥

॥ फलश्रुति ॥
नागस्तोत्रमिदं पुण्यं यः पठेत् सर्पभक्तितः।
न तस्य सर्पभयं किञ्चित् काल-सर्प-दोषहरम्॥

नागपञ्चम्यां विशेषतो नागपूजनं फलदम्।
श्रावणे शुक्लपक्षे तु पञ्चम्यां नागपूजनम्॥

॥ इति श्री नाग स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Naga Stotram ||

|| Dhyanam ||
Phana-mani-vibhushitam Kanaka-varnam-ujjvalam.
Visha-ananam Mahogram Cha Sarparajam Namamyaham.

|| Stotram ||
Sarparajo Mahasarpah Shesho Vasukis-tatha.
Takshako Nagarajash-cha Karkoto-atha Dhananjayah. || 1 ||

Kaliyah Padmanabhash-cha Shankhapalo Mahabalah.
Ete Navanagendrah Sarve Shiva-ganah Smritah. || 2 ||

Anantam Vasukim Shesham Padmanabham Cha Kambalam.
Shankhapalam Dhartarashtram Takshakam Kaliyam Tatha. || 3 ||

Etani Nava-namani Naganam Pratar-utthaya Yah Pathet.
Tasya Naga Bhayam Nasti Sarvatra Vijayi Bhavet. || 4 ||

|| Naga-devata Stuti ||
Namo-astu Sarpebhyo Ye Ke Cha Prithivim-anu.
Ye Antarikashe Ye Divi Tebhyah Sarpebhyo Namah. || 5 ||

Ye Nagah Patale Loke Ye Nagah Sagare Sthitah.
Ye Nagah Parvate Vrikshe Ye Nagah Rishi-sevitah. || 6 ||

Tebhyah Sarvebhyo Namo Namo Nagebhyo Bhujageshvarebhyah.
Naga-loka-patibhyash-cha Tebhyo Nityam Namo Namah. || 7 ||

|| Sheshanaga Stuti ||
Sahasra-shirsham Devam Vishvaksham Vishva-shambhuvam.
Shesham Shantam-anantam Cha Vishnu-shayyam Namamyaham. || 8 ||

Yasy-oragendrasya Shirahsu Prithivi Sthita.
Namami Tam-anantam Cha Shivam Shesham Sumangalam. || 9 ||

|| Naga Puja Mantra ||
Om Namo Bhagavate Vasukaye Namah.
Om Namo Bhagavate Takshakaya Namah.
Om Namo Bhagavate Sheshaya Namah.
Om Namo Bhagavate Karkotakaya Namah.
Om Namo Bhagavate Padmaya Namah.
Om Namo Bhagavate Mahapadmaya Namah.
Om Namo Bhagavate Shankhaya Namah.
Om Namo Bhagavate Kulikaya Namah.

|| Kala Sarpa Shanti Mantra ||
Om Nagebhyo Namah. Om Sarpebhyo Namah.
Sarva-naga-pritaye Sarva-sarpa-bhaya-nivaranaya
Naga-raja-sheshaya Namo Namah.

|| Phalashruti ||
Naga-stotram-idam Punyam Yah Pathet Sarpa-bhaktitah.
Na Tasya Sarpa-bhayam Kinchit Kala-sarpa-dosha-haram.

Naga-panchamyam Visheshato Naga-pujanam Phaladam.
Shravane Shukla-pakshe Tu Panchamyam Naga-pujanam.

|| Iti Shri Naga Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
नाग स्तोत्र में नौ प्रमुख नागेन्द्रों — शेष, वासुकि, तक्षक, कार्कोटक, धनञ्जय, कालिय, पद्मनाभ, शंखपाल और अनन्त — की स्तुति है। ये सभी शिव के गण माने जाते हैं।

प्रातःकाल इन नौ नागों के नामों का पाठ करने से सर्प-भय नहीं रहता और सर्वत्र विजय प्राप्त होती है।

नागदेवता स्तुति में पृथ्वी, अन्तरिक्ष, स्वर्ग, पाताल, सागर, पर्वत और वृक्षों पर निवास करने वाले समस्त नागों को नमन है।

शेषनाग स्तुति में सहस्रशीर्ष, शान्त, अनन्त, विष्णुशय्या शेषनाग को नमन है जिनके शिरों पर पृथ्वी स्थित है।

काल सर्प दोष शान्ति मन्त्र और अष्टनाग पूजा मन्त्र भी संकलित हैं। नागपञ्चमी (श्रावण शुक्ल पञ्चमी) पर इस स्तोत्र का पाठ विशेष फलदायी है।
benefits: Traditionally recited for removal of fear of snakes and protects from snake bites
Invokes the nine great Nagas who are considered Shiva's attendants
Powerful remedy for Kala Sarpa Dosha in the horoscope
Sheshanaga stuti brings stability and cosmic support
Especially potent when recited on Nag Panchami festival
Morning recitation of nine Naga names ensures victory everywhere
description: The Naga Stotram, known in Sanskrit as नाग स्तोत्रम्, is a devotional hymn of praise in praise of Lord Shiva. This revered text originates from Agni Purana, Garuda Purana. Regular recitation is believed to sheshanaga stuti brings stability and cosmic support, remove fear of snakes and protect from snake bites, and invoke the nine great Nagas who are considered Shiva's attendants. This sacred text is ideally recited during Nag Panchami for enhanced blessings. Comprising 9 verses, it can be completed in approximately 10 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Naga Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Naga Stotram in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Nine Naga prayer for snake protection, Kala Sarpa Dosha remedy, and Nag Panchami worship.
relatedSearches: Kaal Sarp dosh Naga stotram remedy

seoFaqs.question: What is the Naga Stotram and which deities does it invoke?
seoFaqs.answer: The Naga Stotram is a Sanskrit hymn invoking the principal serpent deities (nagas) of Hindu cosmology as protective and beneficent forces. Its most circulated form, sometimes called the Navnag Stotra, names nine nagas: Ananta, Vasuki, Shesha, Padmanabha, Kambala, Shankhapala, Dhritarashtra, Takshaka and Kaliya. Each is invoked with epithets describing his cosmic function, with Ananta supporting the worlds, Vasuki serving as the rope of the Mount Mandara churn, Shesha as Vishnu's serpent-couch, and the others presiding over various directions and patala-loka regions. The hymn often opens with a salutation to Shiva, who wears nagas as ornaments, framing the serpent worship within a Shaiva theological context.

seoFaqs.question: How does the Naga Stotram relate to Nag Panchami?
seoFaqs.answer: Nag Panchami, observed on the bright fifth tithi of Shravana month (and also of Bhadrapada in some regional calendars), is the principal annual day of serpent worship across India. The Naga Stotram, especially the Navnag Stotra, is the central textual recitation of the festival, complementing offerings of milk, turmeric, lotus flowers and rice flour to live snakes, snake images and to anthills (which are believed to be naga abodes). In Maharashtra, Karnataka and parts of Andhra Pradesh, the recitation is performed by the female members of the household for the protection of brothers, while in Kerala the festival centres on visits to ancestral sarpa kavus (sacred snake groves).

seoFaqs.question: Is the Naga Stotram a Vedic mantra or a later composition?
seoFaqs.answer: The serpent-praising material in Hindu literature has both Vedic and post-Vedic layers. The Sarpa Sukta of the Rig Veda Khila and the serpent verses in the Atharva Veda are the oldest stratum and use the term sarpa more than naga. The Mahabharata Adi Parva, particularly the Astika Parva, contains extensive naga material. The Navnag Stotra and modern Naga Stotram compilations are later devotional compositions drawing on this Puranic stratum, especially the Bhavishya Purana, the Garuda Purana and the Skanda Purana. The popular short shloka 'Anantam Vasukim Shesham Padmanabham cha Kambalam' is a later anthological verse rather than a single Vedic mantra, though it is treated with full ritual sanctity.

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Naga Stotram traditionally promise?
seoFaqs.answer: The phala-shruti and traditional usage of the Naga Stotram promise four principal benefits. First, protection from snake bites and from fear of snakes for the reciter and his household, especially during the rainy season when snakes are most active. Second, mitigation of Sarpa Dosha and Kaal Sarp Yoga in the natal chart, often as a complement to the more elaborate Kaal Sarp Dosh Nivaran Stotram. Third, blessings for progeny, since nagas are associated with fertility in many regional traditions, particularly in Kerala. Fourth, ancestral peace through propitiation of naga-related pitru karma. Reciting the stotram on Nag Panchami after a head bath and offering of milk to a snake image is the standard household practice.

seoFaqs.question: How does the Naga Stotram differ from the Kaal Sarp Dosh Nivaran Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The Naga Stotram is a universal praise hymn to serpent deities, recited by anyone on Nag Panchami or as a regular protective parayana, regardless of horoscopic condition. The Kaal Sarp Dosh Nivaran Stotram is by contrast a remedial recitation prescribed specifically for natal charts containing the Rahu-Ketu-bracketed planetary configuration called Kaal Sarp Yoga, and is undertaken with a sankalpa for that specific astrological remedy. Structurally, the Naga Stotram lists and praises the nine nagas, while the Nivaran version pairs the same naga invocations with Tantric beeja mantras and a phala-shruti tied to relief from Kaal Sarp effects. Devotees often recite the Naga Stotram first as the standard praise and then proceed to the Nivaran version if astrologically indicated.
